body: Recorded clean in one take after the mic warmup. Brian was sharp on the research versus folklore split. Good anchor for the season.
Enjoyed it. Send me the cut before you publish and I will flag anything I overstated on the meta-analysis.
Solo take done. Kept it to the measurable-driver thesis so it doubles as the trailer spine.
Patricia's levels ran hot on the first pass. Reset her gain and re-recorded the intro. Second take is the keeper.
Great conversation. Happy to come back once the show is live.
Kept every client reference anonymized. Re-listen with that lens before publishing so nothing traces back to Crestline or Hawthorne.
Cut two false starts and leveled room tone. Landed at 29:40, inside the target. Cold open added.
Intro and outro beds exported as standalone stems so we stop re-cutting them weekly. Editing the solo episode over the Friday writing block.
Two guests soft-confirmed from the Discord. Holding tentative slots and will lock calendar once you approve the dates.
Approved both. Aim for weekday mornings after CrossFit, not Friday afternoons. Keep the third slot open for a healthcare-side voice.
Cover art reads clean at thumbnail. Title survives the shrink, which was the whole worry. Exported at 3000x3000.
Feed validated and submitted to Spotify and Apple. Download projections sit well inside the plan tier, so the $300/mo covers us for launch.
Moved the panels six inches behind the mic and the slap echo is gone. Retested with a clap track. Corner is ready.
Shelving the sponsor slot. Chasing ad money before we have download numbers is backwards. Revisit after launch.
Overview reworked so the hook is the measurability claim, not the usual safety sermon. Two pages, tight.
Chapter 1 leans on the anonymized Hawthorne and Vantage evidence. Every claim has a citation behind it before Brian reviews.
Drafting the cost-of-silence chapter over the protected Friday blocks. Keeping it evidence-first; cut two paragraphs that drifted into buzzwords.
Comps assembled with honest recent sales context. We add the measurement angle the shelf is missing without overselling it.
Annotated outline drafted end to end. Sending to Rick so he sees the whole arc, not just the strong open.
Outline reads well. The middle third sags a little; give chapters five and six a clearer throughline before we take it out.
Package is nearly ready. Follow-up call is on the October calendar so this does not drift. Will send once the outline revision lands.
Reading chapter 1 now. Flagged one measurement claim that overstates the effect size; the rest holds up against the literature.
Good catch. I will soften that line to the conservative estimate and add the confidence interval.
